baseline_guidance: Run each eval with this skill and compare against a no-skill baseline or the previous skill version; pass only when the skilled run better satisfies the assertions without losing required safety or precision.

evals.prompt: Build this checkout discount feature test-first. I want red-green-refactor.
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms the public interface and highest-value behaviors, then writes one failing behavior test, implements only enough code to pass it, and repeats vertically.
assertions: Confirms the public interface and priority behaviors before writing code.
Names behaviors rather than implementation steps.
Writes one test at a time and verifies it fails before implementation.
Implements only enough code to pass the current test.
Does not refactor while the suite is red.

evals.prompt: Add tests for this parser by mocking every private helper so we can refactor safely.
evals.expected_output: The agent pushes back on implementation-coupled tests and redirects toward behavior tests through the parser's public interface.
assertions: Rejects testing private helpers or internal collaborators as the main strategy.
Explains that tests should survive internal refactors.
Uses the public interface as the test surface.
Mentions integration-style behavior tests where appropriate.
